title: ECG Monitoring
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- As part of a normal patient assessment: 
- Patients with respiratory or cardiovascular compromise.
- To monitor possible ECG changes during and post treatment.

#### Procedure

- Choose an area of skin away from large muscle mass or excess hair: 
- Clip area as required.

- Clean the area with an alcohol swab.

- Attach the electrodes to the dots and then remove backing, place on patient in the appropriate positions on chest or limbs.

- Ensure the patient is as still as possible.

- Print and record the ECG.

Figure 1

#### Specific Notes

- Artifact can be reduced by ensuring: 
- The patient (or ambulance) is not moving, electric blankets are turned off and the patient takes quiet, regular respirations where possible.
- During removal do not pull leads to remove them from monitoring dots – this can result in the metal electrode plate separating from adhesive pad and becoming stuck in the lead.
